## [00:00 - 03:04]

Alright, this is a really interesting statistic and what I realized when creating this presentation is that The reason that we need to talk about this really doesn't have much to do with like the Logistics of running retargeting campaigns like there are definitely some differences When it comes to cold traffic versus retargeting and we're gonna talk about those a lot of this has to do with mindset and Especially what I was just saying the mindset around something like Scaling a retargeting campaign right or even the function of a retargeting campaign the way that you think about these campaigns is very very different from than it is when we're thinking about cold traffic campaigns and You know on average it takes about eight touch points to make a sale This is a staff that's been around for a long time is still completely relevant And there's probably even more touch points needed nowadays Because consumer behavior is changing in such a big way But this I thought was so important to make this point to you guys because most of us when we're buying media and Especially when we're setting up cold traffic campaigns We are you know running ads and we're you know hopping in after three days Hopping in after five days and saying am I making my money back right? Am I acquiring customers and there's nothing wrong with doing this? But it's important to keep in mind that those people that you're acquiring from Those cold traffic campaigns Those people are rare right those people only needed one Or two touch points to commit to you and to buy from you And so there are all of these other consumers out there in your market That need more touch points with you They need more touch points with your brand to actually decide to make that purchase and this statistic is a perfect representation of that on Average it takes eight touch points to make a sale that doesn't mean that some people won't buy after just one touch point Or just two touch points, but on average It takes eight touch points to make a sale. So if we go back to the analogy that I always use about your market being a pond of fish if You are only running traffic to cold traffic and you're only judging the success of your campaigns Based off of the immediate return, you know, you're reaching this corner up here of your market Right the people that are willing to make that commitment to you after only one or two touch points But what about the rest of your market that needs three four five six seven on average eight?

## [03:04 - 06:05]

Which means there are people that need nine ten eleven twelve twenty thirty Right there are people in your market that require much more than you're giving them if you are only Judging the success of your traffic system Based off of the results of your cold traffic campaigns. So a little myth buster Retargeting doesn't always produce a lower CPA and what I mean by this and we're gonna quickly go back through Ezra's traffic system that we introduced in module one Because it's such a great example of retargeting you will see that as Ezra's traffic system develops His CPAs go down, right? So at the end he's eventually paying, you know, five dollars to acquire a customer Now that doesn't calculate all of the money that he spends in the other campaigns the other seven touch points That he's spent money to reach this person, right? That's being calculated in their own campaigns And so although it appears that the CPA gets cheaper as we go through the customer journey And it does when you're looking at it from a campaign to campaign basis In the end just because you're retargeting doesn't always mean you're going to acquire the Customer for less money. So this is a myth that I see people expect that because you're retargeting You're going to pay a lot less to acquire this person That is absolutely not the case because the people that only need one or two touches Those people are going to be pretty inexpensive to retarget because you know You only really need them to take one click over to your website to take this action But again, what about the other portion of your audience that you might need to pay to show ten different ads to Over a period of time. So this was something I really a point that is so important to understand As we talk about retargeting number one Retargeting isn't always cheaper just because you always already have a relationship with that person Especially if you calculate how much you've spent on each touch point that you've had with that person in the past Another way to think about retargeting another important point that I'm trying to make is that again retargeting isn't always about acquiring Really cheap customers. It's also about scale Because if we go back to the pond of fish analogy if we're only running cold traffic campaigns or only judging the success Of our traffic system based off of how someone Behaves after the first few days that they've interacted with us. ## [33:37 - 36:43]

We are letting them sit there and do their job retargeting campaigns or catchalls, right? So these run all the time and these evergreen campaigns are the campaigns that are moving people From one step of your traffic system to the other, right? Very very simple They're always on and they're always moving people from one step to the other. So for example I'll show you guys here in a minute We set up a custom audience of people that visit asra's free-sell article But don't visit his store. I am taking as a consumer And so these campaigns are there and they're the the audiences are are updating on their own We're not touching anything other than adjusting budget when needed Or maybe throwing in some new ad creatives or copy when we see the frequencies getting high But these evergreen campaigns are meant to run As long as possible And they're there to move people from one step of your traffic system to the other in an automated way because you are using custom audiences so a great example of this again is Here with us we're giving this 10 off discount. So we're targeting people who Visited the cart, right? Retargeting and what you're saying in your ad Obviously what you're telling them to do is to take the next step, right? What is someone who's being retargeted? It's someone who's shown interest in something but didn't take the action So if we can use testimonials and our ad copies for retargeting That might convince them to take that step Because they see that it's worked for someone else, right or that it's worked for someone like them Overcoming barriers to purchase that is key With retargeting and a great use of your ad copy What I will do when I start working with a brand is ask their customer service What are your top frequently asked questions? I'll then take whatever I think, you know, which ones are the best or the most relevant And turn those into ad copies. So the example I used earlier shoes I was working with this company selling shoes And a big question is are they waterproof? And so I ran an ad that was really simple Hey, you know, you might not know but these shoes are waterproof Which means, you know, you can go on so many more adventures and your socks won't get wet your feet won't get wet So looking at what are barriers to purchase a lot of times it's cost So for example at digital marketer, we would make analogies. Oh, you know, it's only seven dollars You can either have a guide to building a landing page Or an expensive cup of coffee, you know When you start to put the cost of something Into perspective For that person, it can be a great way to overcome barriers to purchase like I know you sell essential oils And think about we've talked about this before but all of the different things you can replace with essential oils like Different cleaners around your home that you're using face, you know, body products skincare products You could create a retargeting ad that you know talks about how much money you could save even though this is an expensive purchase Well, look at all the money you can save just right by replacing these certain products and oh, it's better for you, right? That's huge For example, right now Ezra is using some language around being cruelty free and That, you know, their products aren't tested on animals

## [58:08 - 1:01:10]

This one's 14 days um So you really should never have a huge frequency issue Because people are automatically moving in and out Of these campaigns either based off of time or based off of actions that they take So if they go ahead and buy then they're not going to see this messenger ad any longer Um and even when it comes to monetization campaigns, you're only running these for you know a week at the longest So you really never should have a crazy frequency issue with retargeting If you're setting this up correctly if you are then you definitely want to look at your budgets Which we'll talk about here in a minute.
